question: How much does the Routes API cost per request?
answer:
The Routes API uses SKU-based pricing with three tiers based on your chosen routing preference [Source 1]:

| SKU | Routing Preference | Price per 1,000 requests |
|---|---|---|
| Compute Routes Basic | `TRAFFIC_UNAWARE` | $5.00 |
| Compute Routes Advanced | `TRAFFIC_AWARE` | $10.00 |
| Compute Routes Preferred | `TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL` | $15.00 |

The same tiering applies to the **Route Matrix** endpoint (`$5.00` / `$10.00` per 1,000 for Basic / Advanced respectively) [Source 1].

Keep in mind that all Google Maps Platform accounts receive a **$200 USD monthly credit** applied automatically, which at the Basic tier covers roughly **40,000 Directions API calls** before any charges apply [Source 1].

A few cost tips:

- **Use field masks** — The Routes API bills based on what fields you request, so only specify what you need via the `X-Goog-FieldMask` header [Source 2].
- **Choose the right tier** — Only use `TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL` if you genuinely need the highest accuracy; `TRAFFIC_UNAWARE` is 3× cheaper for use cases where real-time traffic doesn't matter [Source 1].
- **Set quota limits** in Cloud Console to prevent unexpected overages [Source 1].

**Sources:**
- [Google Maps Platform Billing and Pricing](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/pricing)
- [Routes API and Directions](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/routes/overview)

judge_notes.mentions: The answer directly addresses the Routes API concept by providing detailed pricing information including SKU-based tiers, costs per request, and practical cost optimization tips.

question: Does Google Maps Platform still offer a $200 monthly credit?
answer:
Yes, Google Maps Platform still offers a $200 USD monthly credit [Source 1]. It's applied automatically to every Google Cloud billing account with Maps APIs enabled — you don't need to do anything to claim it. The credit covers all Maps Platform APIs (Maps, Routes, and Places) and resets on the first of each month, but it cannot be accumulated or rolled over [Source 1].

At typical usage levels, the $200 credit covers approximately [Source 1]:
- **28,500** Dynamic Maps loads
- **40,000** Directions API calls
- **10,000** Place Details calls

One important note: even if your usage stays within the free credit, you still need a valid billing account (credit card or bank account) linked to your Google Cloud project. Without one, your APIs will stop working and you'll see a `BillingNotEnabledMapError` [Source 2].

**Sources:**
- [Google Maps Platform Billing and Pricing](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/pricing)
